title: METAL GEAR SOLID V: THE PHANTOM PAIN

notes: Mouse issue is definitely present in the  Map Screen. However, it can be drastically improved by using protontricks's winecfg to run the game in a virtual desktop matching the desired resolution, as well as lowering the mouse's polling rate. Additionally, running the game with Shadows, Lighting and Ambient Occulsion set to "Extra High" appears to create occasional microstutter and frame drops, which disappear with those settings set to "High".
title: METAL GEAR SOLID V: THE PHANTOM PAIN

notes: Runs perfectly, but mouse input lags on map and in cut-scenes.
title: METAL GEAR SOLID V: THE PHANTOM PAIN

notes: Worked fine out of the box, as others have mentioned there are issues with the GPS navigation on iDroid however it did not pose an issue for functionality in my case (could always select drop locations even though mouse tracking was extremely poor). Rest of the game ran flawlessly. If others are experiencing issues with iDroid would experiment with very high sensitivity (perhaps setting up the opposite of a sniper button if it proves successful).

title: METAL GEAR SOLID V: THE PHANTOM PAIN

notes:
Game itself installs and runs fairly well with comparable to native performance. Screen Tearing is pretty pervasive but not particularly severe. Overall fully playable and enjoyable even with a handful of noticeable bugs. 

Main issues involve action prompts being a mix of keyboard and controller prompts even when no controller is plugged in, and extremely poor mouse tracking in the iDroid menus, radial menus, and some cutscenes, but in the majority of cases it's only a minor annoyance.

Connects to servers and singleplayer online elements are fully functional, but the connection can be unstable. Most of the time if a connection error occurs, simply trying again will have it work and in
title: METAL GEAR SOLID V: THE PHANTOM PAIN
